subject: Python version support
truth: Stop using CPython 2.6, CPython 3.3, or Jython 2.7; they are no longer supported.
quote: Support for CPython 2.6, CPython 3.3 and Jython 2.7 has been dropped.

subject: Deprecated variables, methods, classes, and backwards-compatibility shims
old: Deprecated variables, methods, classes, and backwards-compatibility shims for old method signatures
truth: Stop using all previously deprecated variables, methods, classes, and backwards-compatibility shims for old method signatures; they have been removed.
quote: Deprecated variables, methods, and classes have been removed, along with all backwards-compatibility shims for old method signatures.

from_fact: deprecated variables, methods, classes, and backwards-compatibility shims for old method signatures

subject: Request option defaults
new: New defaults based on community feedback
truth: The defaults for a number of request options have changed; review your configuration to ensure expected behaviour.
quote: We also changed the defaults for a number of request options based on community feedback.

from_fact: new defaults based on community feedback

subject: Response.set_header, Response.delete_header, Response.get_header, Response.set_headers
old: could be used to set raw Set-Cookie headers (silently producing incorrect responses for multiple cookies)
new: raise ValueError when used for Set-Cookie
truth: Stop using set_header, delete_header, get_header, or set_headers to manipulate Set-Cookie headers; they now raise ValueError.
quote: could be used to attempt to set raw cookie headers.

from_fact: could be used to set raw set-cookie headers (silently producing incorrect responses for multiple cookies)
raise valueerror when used for set-cookie

subject: falcon.testing.Result.json
old: raised an error when response body was empty
new: returns None when response body is empty
truth: Stop expecting falcon.testing.Result.json to raise an error on empty body; it now returns None.
quote: falcon.testing.Result.json now returns None when the response body is empty, rather than raising an error.

from_fact: raised an error when response body was empty
returns none when response body is empty

subject: Request.get_param_as_bool
new: treats valueless parameters as truthy
truth: Stop relying on get_param_as_bool treating valueless parameters as falsy; they are now treated as truthy.
quote: Request.get_param_as_bool now defaults to treating valueless parameters as truthy, rather than falsy.

subject: Request.get_param_as_bool with blank_as_true=False
old: raised an error for a valueless parameter when blank_as_true=False
new: returns False for a valueless parameter when blank_as_true=False
truth: Stop catching errors from get_param_as_bool with blank_as_true=False on valueless parameters; it now returns False instead of raising.
quote: Request.get_param_as_bool no longer raises an error for a valueless parameter when the blank_as_true keyword argument is False. Instead, False is simply returned in that case.

from_fact: raised an error for a valueless parameter when blank_as_true=false
returns false for a valueless parameter when blank_as_true=false

subject: RequestOptions.keep_blank_qs_values
new: defaults to True
truth: Stop assuming blank query string values are dropped by default; keep_blank_qs_values now defaults to True.
quote: RequestOptions.keep_blank_qs_values now defaults to True instead of False.

subject: RequestOptions.auto_parse_qs_csv
new: defaults to False
truth: Stop relying on query string CSV auto-parsing by default; auto_parse_qs_csv now defaults to False.
quote: RequestOptions.auto_parse_qs_csv now defaults to False instead of True.

subject: Request.context_type
new: bare class implementing mapping interface
truth: Stop treating Request.context_type as a plain dict; it is now a bare class with a mapping interface.
quote: Request :attr:`~falcon.Request.context_type` was changed from dict to a bare class

subject: Response.context_type
new: bare class implementing mapping interface
truth: Stop treating Response.context_type as a plain dict; it is now a bare class with a mapping interface.
quote: Response :attr:`~falcon.Response.context_type` was changed from dict to a bare class

subject: JSONHandler and HTTPError JSON serialization
new: use standard json library; JSONHandler configurable with custom dumps/loads
truth: Stop relying on ujson being used automatically by JSONHandler or HTTPError; JSONHandler now uses standard json by default and can be configured with custom dumps/loads.
quote: `ujson` in lieu of the standard `json` library (when `ujson` is available in

from_fact: use standard json library; jsonhandler configurable with custom dumps/loads

subject: custom router find() method
old: could omit req keyword argument (backwards-compatible shim existed)
new: must accept req keyword argument
truth: Stop using a custom router find() method that doesn't accept the req keyword argument; the backwards-compatible shim was removed.
quote: The find() method for a custom router is now required to accept the req keyword argument that was added in a previous release. The backwards-compatible shim was removed.

from_fact: could omit req keyword argument (backwards-compatible shim existed)

subject: middleware methods and hooks
old: could accept fewer arguments (backwards-compatible shims existed)
new: must accept arguments as specified in Falcon 2.0 interface definitions
truth: Stop using middleware or hooks that don't match the exact Falcon 2.0 argument signatures; all backwards-compatible shims were removed.
quote: now accept the arguments as specified in the relevant interface definitions

from_fact: could accept fewer arguments (backwards-compatible shims existed)
must accept arguments as specified in falcon 2.0 interface definitions

subject: custom error serializers
old: could accept different arguments (backwards-compatible shim existed)
new: must accept arguments as specified by set_error_serializer
truth: Stop using custom error serializers that don't match the exact argument signature from set_error_serializer; the backwards-compatible shim was removed.
quote: Custom error serializers are now required to accept the arguments as

from_fact: could accept different arguments (backwards-compatible shim existed)
must accept arguments as specified by set_error_serializer

subject: make_router_search() function
truth: Stop using make_router_search() from api_helpers; it has been removed.
quote: An internal function, make_router_search(), was removed from the api_helpers module.

subject: wrap_old_error_serializer() function
truth: Stop using wrap_old_error_serializer() from api_helpers; it has been removed.
quote: An internal function, wrap_old_error_serializer(), was removed from the api_helpers module.

subject: Request.headers and Request.cookies properties
new: return a direct reference to an internal cached object
truth: Stop mutating the objects returned by Request.headers or Request.cookies; they now return a direct reference to an internal cached object instead of a copy.
quote: an internal cached object, rather than making a copy each time.

from_fact: return a direct reference to an internal cached object

subject: Request.stream
old: was wrapped in a bounded stream on wsgiref server
new: no longer wrapped in a bounded stream on wsgiref server
truth: Stop relying on Request.stream being a bounded stream on wsgiref; use Request.bounded_stream instead for normalized stream semantics.
quote: The :attr:`falcon.Request.stream` attribute is no longer wrapped in a bounded

from_fact: was wrapped in a bounded stream on wsgiref server
no longer wrapped in a bounded stream on wsgiref server

subject: Request.cookies precedence
old: gave precedence to the last value for a given cookie name
new: gives precedence to the first value for a given cookie name
truth: Stop relying on Request.cookies returning the last value for duplicate cookie names; it now returns the first value.
quote: Request.cookies now gives precedence to the first value encountered in the Cookie header for a given cookie name, rather than the last.

from_fact: gave precedence to the last value for a given cookie name
gives precedence to the first value for a given cookie name

subject: error handler signature
new: handle_error(req, resp, ex, params)
truth: Error handler callbacks must now accept (req, resp, ex, params) instead of (ex, req, resp, params).

subject: strip_url_path_trailing_slash default
new: False
truth: RequestOptions.strip_url_path_trailing_slash now defaults to False instead of True, so trailing slashes are no longer stripped by default.
quote: :attr:`~.falcon.RequestOptions.strip_url_path_trailing_slash` now defaults

subject: router additional args acceptance
old: routers could accept additional args and kwargs
new: routers only accept additional options as variadic keyword arguments
truth: Routers are now only allowed to accept additional options as variadic keyword arguments and must ignore unsupported arguments.
quote: Routers were previously allowed to accept additional args and keyword arguments, and were not required to use the variadic form. Now, they are only allowed to accept additional options as variadic keyword arguments, and to ignore any arguments they don't support.

from_fact: routers could accept additional args and kwargs
routers only accept additional options as variadic keyword arguments

subject: API.add_route() args
new: API.add_route() no longer accepts *args
truth: API.add_route() previously accepted *args but now no longer does.
quote: :meth:`~.API.add_route` previously accepted `*args`, but now no longer does.

subject: parse_query_string keyword arguments
new: keep_blank, csv
truth: parse_query_string keyword arguments were renamed: keep_blank_qs_values → keep_blank, parse_qs_csv → csv.
quote:
# Before
parsed_values = parse_query_string(
    query_string, keep_blank_qs_values=True, parse_qs_csv=False
)

# Falcon 2.0
parsed_values = parse_query_string(
    query_string, keep_blank=True, csv=False
)

subject: get_param_as_int keyword arguments
new: min_value, max_value
truth: get_param_as_int keyword arguments were renamed: min → min_value, max → max_value to avoid shadowing built-in names.
quote:
# Before
dpr = req.get_param_as_int('dpr', min=0, max=3)

# Falcon 2.0
dpr = req.get_param_as_int('dpr', min_value=0, max_value=3)

subject: Request cookie parsing
old: used standard library for cookie parsing
new: custom RFC 6265-based parser
truth: Request cookie parsing now uses a custom RFC 6265-based parser instead of the standard library, which may produce different results for archaic cookie header formats.
quote: Request cookie parsing no longer uses the standard library for most of the parsing logic. This may lead to subtly different results for archaic cookie header formats, since the new implementation is based on RFC 6265.

from_fact: used standard library for cookie parsing

subject: Request.if_match and Request.if_none_match
new: return list of falcon.ETag objects
truth: Request.if_match and Request.if_none_match now return a list of falcon.ETag objects instead of the raw header string value.
quote: The :attr:`~falcon.Request.if_match` and :attr:`~falcon.Request.if_none_match` properties now return a list of :class:`falcon.ETag` objects rather than the raw value of the If-Match or If-None-Match headers, respectively.

subject: Response.etag property setter
new: wraps value with double-quotes if not already present
truth: Setting Response.etag now wraps the value with double-quotes if not already present, to comply with RFC 7232.
quote: When setting the :attr:`~falcon.Response.etag` header property, the value will now be wrapped with double-quotes (if not already present) to ensure compliance with RFC 7232.

from_fact: wraps value with double-quotes if not already present

subject: Default error serializer Content-Type charset
new: no charset parameter in Content-Type
truth: The default error serializer no longer sets the charset parameter in the Content-Type header, which may break test assertions that check the exact Content-Type value.
quote: The default error serializer no longer sets the `charset` parameter for the media type returned in the Content-Type header, since UTF-8 is the default encoding for both JSON and XML media types. This should not break well-behaved clients, but could impact test cases in apps that assert on the exact value of the Content-Type header.

subject: Default JSON media type constants and defaults
old: included charset parameter in media type
new: no charset parameter in media type
truth: falcon.DEFAULT_MEDIA_TYPE, falcon.MEDIA_JSON, the default media_type for falcon.API, and default_media_type options no longer include the charset parameter.
quote: the default JSON media type generally used for successful responses was also modified to no longer specify the `charset` parameter. This change affects both the :data:`falcon.DEFAULT_MEDIA_TYPE` and :data:`falcon.MEDIA_JSON` :ref:`constants <media_type_constants>`, as well as the default value of the `media_type` keyword argument specified for the :class:`falcon.API` initializer. This change also affects the default value of the :attr:`.RequestOptions.default_media_type` and :attr:`.ResponseOptions.default_media_type` options.

from_fact: included charset parameter in media type
no charset parameter in media type

subject: Request.context_type
new: attribute-based context (req.context.key)
truth: Request.context_type now defaults to a bare class; the dict-style interface (req.context['key']) is deprecated in favor of attribute access (req.context.key).
quote: Request :attr:`~falcon.Request.context_type` was changed from dict to a bare class

subject: Response.context_type
new: defaults to a bare class
truth: Response.context_type now defaults to a bare class instead of dict; use resp.context.cache_strategy = 'lru' instead of resp.context['cache_strategy'] = 'lru'.
quote: Response :attr:`~falcon.Response.context_type` now defaults to a bare class allowing to set attributes on the response context object

subject: error handler parameter order
new: def handle_error(req, resp, ex, params)
truth: Custom error handlers now receive (req, resp, ex, params) instead of (ex, req, resp, params).
quote: The ordering of the parameters passed to custom error handlers was adjusted to be more intuitive and consistent with the rest of the framework

subject: Response.set_stream / stream_len
new: Response.content_length property
truth: Stop using stream_len on Response; use content_length or set_stream instead.
quote: Please use :meth:`~falcon.Response.set_stream()` or :attr:`~falcon.Response.content_length` instead.

subject: media handler serialize/deserialize signatures
new: deserialize(stream, content_type, content_length) and serialize(content_type)
truth: Media handler deserialize() now takes stream, content_type, content_length instead of a single raw argument; serialize() now receives a content_type argument.
quote: The serialize() media handler method now receives an extra content_type argument, while the deserialize() method now takes stream, content_type, and content_length arguments, rather than a single raw argument.

subject: Request.if_match and if_none_match return type
new: list of falcon.ETag objects
truth: Request.if_match and Request.if_none_match now return a list of falcon.ETag objects instead of the raw header string.
quote: now return a list of :class:`falcon.ETag` objects rather than the raw

subject: default error serializer Content-Type charset
new: Content-Type header no longer includes charset parameter
truth: The default error serializer no longer sets the charset parameter in the Content-Type header for error responses.
quote: The default error serializer no longer sets the charset parameter for the media type returned in the Content-Type header, since UTF-8 is the default encoding for both JSON and XML media types.

from_fact: content-type header no longer includes charset parameter

subject: default JSON media type / DEFAULT_MEDIA_TYPE / MEDIA_JSON constants
old: media type strings included charset parameter
new: media type strings no longer include charset parameter
truth: falcon.DEFAULT_MEDIA_TYPE, falcon.MEDIA_JSON, and the default media_type in API, RequestOptions, and ResponseOptions no longer include the charset parameter.
quote: The default error serializer no longer sets the `charset` parameter for the

from_fact: media type strings included charset parameter
media type strings no longer include charset parameter

subject: CaseInsensitiveDict inheritance (Python 3)
new: inherits from collections.abc.MutableMapping
truth: On Python 3, CaseInsensitiveDict now inherits from collections.abc.MutableMapping instead of collections.MutableMapping; code that relies on the old MRO or isinstance checks against collections.MutableMapping may break.
quote: The :class:`falcon.CaseInsensitiveDict` class now inherits from :class:`collections.abc.MutableMapping` under Python 3, instead of :class:`collections.MutableMapping`.

subject: Response.etag property wrapping
new: etag value wrapped with double-quotes if not already present
truth: Setting Response.etag now wraps the value in double-quotes (if not already present) to comply with RFC 7232; code that manually adds quotes or compares etag values may break.
quote: When setting the :attr:`~falcon.Response.etag` header property, the value will now be wrapped with double-quotes (if not already present) to ensure compliance with RFC 7232.

from_fact: etag value wrapped with double-quotes if not already present

subject: simulate_request header values on Python 2
old: header values could be non-str types
new: header values forced to str on Python 2
truth: On Python 2, simulate_request now forces header values to str as required by PEP-3333; passing non-str header values that were previously accepted will now be coerced or may behave differently.
quote: The :meth:`~falcon.testing.TestClient.simulate_request` method now forces header values to `str` on Python 2 as required by PEP-3333.

from_fact: header values could be non-str types
header values forced to str on python 2

subject: custom error serializers for HTTPError with has_representation=False
old: custom error serializers not called for HTTPError instances where has_representation is False
new: custom error serializers called for all HTTPError instances
truth: Custom error serializers are now called for all HTTPError instances, including those where has_representation is False (e.g. subclasses of NoRepresentation); previously they were skipped.
quote: that custom error serializers will be called for all instances of

from_fact: custom error serializers not called for httperror instances where has_representation is false
custom error serializers called for all httperror instances

subject: Python 3.4 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.4; Falcon 3.0 requires Python 3.5+ (WSGI) or 3.6+ (ASGI).
quote: Python 3.4 is no longer supported.

subject: CPython 2.7 and PyPy2.7 support
truth: Stop using CPython 2.7 or PyPy2.7; Falcon 3.0 removed Python 2 support entirely.
quote: support for CPython 2.7 and PyPy2.7 was removed in Falcon 3.0.

subject: OptionalRepresentation class
truth: Stop using OptionalRepresentation; the default error serializer now always generates a representation for every HTTPError subclass.
quote: The class :class:`~.falcon.http_error.OptionalRepresentation` and the attribute

subject: HTTPError.has_representation attribute
truth: Stop using has_representation; all HTTPError subclasses now always have a representation generated by the default serializer.
quote: subclasses of :class:`falcon.HTTPError` now have a media type representation.

subject: NoRepresentation class
truth: Stop using NoRepresentation; all HTTPError subclasses now have a media type representation.
quote: subclasses of :class:`falcon.HTTPError` now have a media type representation.

subject: Response body clearing on exception
old: Previously set response body was preserved when handling raised exceptions
new: Falcon now clears any previously set response body before handling any raised exception
truth: Any response body set before an exception is raised will now be cleared before the error handler runs.
quote: Falcon now ensures that any previously set response body is cleared before handling any raised exception.

from_fact: previously set response body was preserved when handling raised exceptions
falcon now clears any previously set response body before handling any raised exception

subject: falcon.testing.create_environ
new: default User-Agent header is now f'falcon-client/{falcon.__version__}'
truth: Stop relying on the default User-Agent being 'curl/7.24.0...'; it is now 'falcon-client/{version}' unless you override falcon.testing.helpers.DEFAULT_UA.
quote: :func:`falcon.testing.create_environ` previously set a default User-Agent header, when one

from_fact: default user-agent header is now f'falcon-client/{falcon.__version__}'

subject: falcon.testing.create_environ
new: http_version keyword argument (value is just version number, no 'HTTP/' prefix)
truth: Stop using protocol=; use http_version= and pass only the version number without 'HTTP/' prefix.
quote: to `http_version` and now only includes the version number (the value is no longer prefixed

from_fact: http_version keyword argument (value is just version number, no 'http/' prefix)

subject: falcon.testing.create_environ
new: root_path keyword argument
truth: Stop using app=; use root_path= instead.
quote: The :func:`falcon.testing.create_environ` function's `app` keyword argument was renamed to `root_path`.

subject: falcon.stream.BoundedStream
new: writable property
truth: Stop using .writeable; use .writable instead.
quote: The `writeable` property of :class:`falcon.stream.BoundedStream` was renamed to `writable` per the standard file-like I/O interface (the old name was a misspelling)

subject: error handler raising non-HTTPStatus/non-HTTPError exceptions
old: remaining middleware process_response methods would still execute before bubbling up
new: remaining middleware process_response methods will no longer execute before bubbling up
truth: If an error handler raises an exception that is not HTTPStatus or HTTPError, remaining middleware process_response methods are skipped before the exception reaches the web server.
quote: If an error handler raises an exception type other than :class:`falcon.HTTPStatus` or :class:`falcon.HTTPError`, remaining middleware `process_response` methods will no longer be executed before bubbling up the unhandled exception to the web server.

from_fact: remaining middleware process_response methods would still execute before bubbling up
remaining middleware process_response methods will no longer execute before bubbling up

subject: falcon.get_http_status
new: no longer accepts floats (method deprecated)
truth: Stop passing floats to falcon.get_http_status; it no longer accepts them and the function itself is deprecated.
quote: :func:`falcon.get_http_status` no longer accepts floats, and the method itself is deprecated.

subject: falcon.app_helpers.prepare_middleware
new: must be passed an iterable
truth: Stop passing a single middleware object to prepare_middleware; the argument must be an iterable.
quote: :func:`falcon.app_helpers.prepare_middleware` no longer accepts a single object; the value that is passed must be an iterable.

subject: falcon.Request.access_route
old: did not include remote_addr as last element
new: now includes remote_addr as last element if not already present in checked headers
truth: access_route now appends remote_addr as the last element if it isn't already in the route from headers.
quote: :attr:`falcon.Request.access_route` now includes the value of the :attr:`~falcon.Request.remote_addr` property as the last element in the route, if not already present in one of the headers that are checked.

from_fact: did not include remote_addr as last element
now includes remote_addr as last element if not already present in checked headers

subject: Unhandled exception behavior
old: unhandled exception raised to web server, middleware process_response skipped
new: default error handler generates HTTP 500, middleware process_response still called
truth: Unhandled exceptions now generate an HTTP 500 response via a default error handler instead of being raised to the web server, and middleware process_response methods are still called.
quote: This also means that middleware `process_response` methods will still be called in this case, rather than being skipped as previously.

from_fact: unhandled exception raised to web server, middleware process_response skipped
default error handler generates http 500, middleware process_response still called

subject: Exception handler resolution order
old: handlers matched in reverse order of registration
new: handlers matched by most specific exception class via MRO
truth: Exception handlers are now resolved by the most specific matching exception class (MRO) instead of reverse registration order.
quote: Exceptions are now handled by the registered handler for the most specific matching exception class, rather than in reverse order of registration.

from_fact: handlers matched in reverse order of registration
handlers matched by most specific exception class via mro

subject: strip_url_path_trailing_slash behavior
old: trailing slash always removed regardless of option
new: trailing slash only removed when option is enabled, routes should be added without trailing slash
truth: When strip_url_path_trailing_slash is enabled, routes must now be added without a trailing slash; the old bug that always stripped it is fixed.
quote: should now be added without a trailing slash.

from_fact: trailing slash always removed regardless of option
trailing slash only removed when option is enabled, routes should be added without trailing slash

subject: Handler exceptions in media handling
new: exceptions wrapped as MediaMalformedError rendered as 400
truth: Exceptions raised by media handlers are now wrapped as MediaMalformedError and rendered as a 400 Bad Request response.
quote: Exceptions raised by the handlers are wrapped as

from_fact: exceptions wrapped as mediamalformederror rendered as 400

subject: Repeated get_media/media after error
old: returned None on subsequent calls after error
new: re-raises the same exception on subsequent calls (unless MediaNotFoundError with default)
truth: Subsequent calls to get_media or media after an error re-raise the same exception instead of returning None.
quote: re-raise the same exception, if the first call ended in an error, unless the

from_fact: returned none on subsequent calls after error
re-raises the same exception on subsequent calls (unless medianotfounderror with default)

subject: falcon.Response.data
old: falcon.Response.data used to also check and serialize falcon.Response.media
new: falcon.Response.data now simply returns the same data object it was set to, ignoring media
truth: Stop relying on falcon.Response.data to serialize media; use falcon.Response.render_body() instead to get the HTTP response body that accounts for text, data, and media.
quote: The :attr:`falcon.Response.data` property now just simply returns the same data

from_fact: falcon.response.data used to also check and serialize falcon.response.media
falcon.response.data now simply returns the same data object it was set to, ignoring media

subject: falcon.util.json import
new: use standard library json or another json library
truth: Stop importing json from falcon.util; it is deprecated.
quote: Importing json from falcon.util is deprecated.

from_fact: use standard library json or another json library

subject: JSONHandler private attributes
old: old private attribute names on JSONHandler
new: renamed private attributes on JSONHandler
truth: Stop referencing old private attribute names on falcon.media.JSONHandler; they have been renamed.
quote: The private attributes for :class:`~.falcon.media.JSONHandler` were renamed, and

from_fact: old private attribute names on jsonhandler

subject: MessagePackHandler private attributes
new: replaced private attributes on MessagePackHandler
truth: Stop referencing old private attributes on falcon.media.MessagePackHandler; they have been replaced.
quote: the private attributes used by :class:`~.falcon.media.MessagePackHandler` were

subject: falcon.media.Handlers.find_by_media_type
truth: Stop using falcon.media.Handlers.find_by_media_type(); it is deprecated and may be removed in a future release.
quote: method was deprecated and may be removed in a future release.

subject: No-route-match exception type
new: falcon.HTTPRouteNotFound (subclass of HTTPNotFound)
truth: When no route matches, the framework now raises HTTPRouteNotFound instead of plain HTTPNotFound, so custom error handlers that catch HTTPNotFound may not catch the new subclass unless they catch the parent.
quote: When no route matches a request, the framework will now raise a specialized subclass of :class:`~.falcon.HTTPNotFound` (:class:`~.falcon.HTTPRouteNotFound`) so that a custom error handler can distinguish that specific case if desired.

subject: RequestOptions.auto_parse_form_urlencoded option
new: falcon.media.URLEncodedFormHandler
truth: Stop using RequestOptions.auto_parse_form_urlencoded; use URLEncodedFormHandler instead. The old option is deprecated.
quote: The :attr:`~.RequestOptions.auto_parse_form_urlencoded` option is now deprecated in favor of :class:`~falcon.media.URLEncodedFormHandler`.

subject: CompiledRouter trailing slash handling
old: CompiledRouter stripped trailing slashes from URI templates, making `/path` and `/path/` map to the same route
new: CompiledRouter now preserves trailing slashes, so `/path` and `/path/` can be separate routes
truth: Stop relying on CompiledRouter stripping trailing slashes; routes with and without trailing slashes are now distinct.
quote: was erroneously stripping trailing slashes from URI templates.

from_fact: compiledrouter stripped trailing slashes from uri templates, making `/path` and `/path/` map to the same route
compiledrouter now preserves trailing slashes, so `/path` and `/path/` can be separate routes

subject: auto_parse_qs_csv query string parsing
old: When auto_parse_qs_csv was enabled, `t=1,2&t=3,4` produced `['1', '2', '3,4']` (only split first occurrence)
new: Now `t=1,2&t=3,4` produces `['1', '2', '3', '4']` (all occurrences are split)
truth: Stop relying on auto_parse_qs_csv only splitting the first occurrence of a repeated query parameter; all occurrences are now split on commas.
quote: now correctly parses all occurrences of the same parameter in the query string,

from_fact: when auto_parse_qs_csv was enabled, `t=1,2&t=3,4` produced `['1', '2', '3,4']` (only split first occurrence)

subject: falcon.uri.encode and encode_value percent escaping
old: falcon.uri.encode and encode_value did not re-escape already-escaped percent characters
new: Now they escape all percent characters by default, even if already escaped
truth: Stop relying on falcon.uri.encode and encode_value preserving already-escaped percent characters; they now double-escape them by default. Use encode_check_escaped or encode_value_check_escaped to retain old behavior.
quote: percent characters by default even if it appears they have already been escaped.

from_fact: falcon.uri.encode and encode_value did not re-escape already-escaped percent characters
now they escape all percent characters by default, even if already escaped

subject: falcon.uri.decode and parse_query_string performance
old: falcon.uri.decode and parse_query_string could explode quadratically for many percent-encoded characters
new: Time complexity is now always close to O(n)
truth: Stop relying on these functions being slow for large numbers of percent-encoded characters; they are now linear time.
quote: explode quadratically for a large number of percent-encoded characters.

from_fact: falcon.uri.decode and parse_query_string could explode quadratically for many percent-encoded characters
time complexity is now always close to o(n)

subject: parse_query_string empty string handling
new: parse_query_string('') now returns {}
truth: Stop relying on parse_query_string('') returning something other than an empty dict; it now returns {}.
quote: empty, rather than raising an error.

subject: response serialization error handling
old: Response serialization errors (faulty media handler, HTTPUnsupportedMediaType) bubbled up to the application server
new: These errors are now handled like other exceptions raised in a responder
truth: Stop relying on response serialization errors propagating to the application server; they are now caught and handled internally like other responder exceptions.
quote: Previously, response serialization errors (such as in the case of a faulty

from_fact: response serialization errors (faulty media handler, httpunsupportedmediatype) bubbled up to the application server
these errors are now handled like other exceptions raised in a responder

subject: Request.forwarded_host port inclusion
old: forwarded_host did not include port when proxy headers were not set
new: forwarded_host now includes the port when proxy headers are not set
truth: Stop relying on forwarded_host omitting the port when not behind a proxy; it now includes the port.
quote: falcon.Request.forwarded_host now contains the port when proxy headers are not set, to make it possible to correctly reconstruct the URL when the application is not behind a proxy.

from_fact: forwarded_host did not include port when proxy headers were not set
forwarded_host now includes the port when proxy headers are not set

subject: Response.downloadable_as non-ASCII filename encoding
old: downloadable_as did not properly encode non-ASCII filenames per RFC 6266
new: downloadable_as now correctly encodes non-ASCII filenames per RFC 6266
truth: Stop relying on downloadable_as encoding non-ASCII filenames in a non-RFC-6266 way; it now follows RFC 6266 recommendations.
quote: is now correctly encoding non-ASCII filenames as per

from_fact: downloadable_as did not properly encode non-ascii filenames per rfc 6266
downloadable_as now correctly encodes non-ascii filenames per rfc 6266

subject: CompiledRouter route parameter setting on non-matching routes
old: CompiledRouter mistakenly set route parameters while exploring non-matching routes
new: CompiledRouter no longer sets route parameters on non-matching routes
truth: Stop relying on route parameters being set during exploration of non-matching routes; they are now only set on matching routes.
quote: The :class:`falcon.routing.CompiledRouter` no longer mistakenly sets route parameters

from_fact: compiledrouter mistakenly set route parameters while exploring non-matching routes
compiledrouter no longer sets route parameters on non-matching routes

subject: to_query_str and simulate_request parameter encoding
old: to_query_str did not correctly encode parameter keys and values, so simulate_request passed special characters like '&' incorrectly
new: to_query_str now correctly encodes parameter keys and values
truth: Stop relying on to_query_str passing special characters like '&' unencoded; keys and values are now properly encoded.
quote: The :func:`~falcon.to_query_str` method now correctly encodes parameter keys

from_fact: to_query_str did not correctly encode parameter keys and values, so simulate_request passed special characters like '&' incorrectly
to_query_str now correctly encodes parameter keys and values

subject: falcon.Request.add_link()
new: falcon.Response.append_link()
truth: Stop using falcon.Request.add_link() and use falcon.Response.append_link() instead.
quote: The `add_link()` method of the :class:`falcon.Request` class was renamed to :meth:`falcon.Response.append_link`. The old name is still available as a deprecated alias.

subject: falcon.HTTPError subclasses
old: positional arguments for optional kwargs of falcon.HTTPError subclasses
new: keyword-only arguments for optional kwargs
truth: Stop passing optional keyword arguments as positional arguments to falcon.HTTPError subclasses; use keyword arguments instead.
quote: Deprecate the use of positional arguments for the optional kw args of the :class:`falcon.HTTPError` subclasses

from_fact: positional arguments for optional kwargs of falcon.httperror subclasses

subject: falcon.API independent_middleware kwarg
new: defaults to True
truth: Stop assuming middleware runs in the old order; independent_middleware now defaults to True.
quote: :attr:`~falcon.RequestOptions.keep_blank_qs_values` now defaults to ``True``

subject: Response.stream_len
new: content_length property (stream_len deprecated)
truth: Stop using Response.stream_len; use Response.content_length or Response.set_stream instead.
quote: The deprecated ``stream_len`` property was removed from the :class:`~falcon.Response` class.

subject: falcon.testing.TestCase.api property
truth: The deprecated falcon.testing.TestCase.api property was removed.
quote: The deprecated ``falcon.testing.TestCase.api`` property was removed.

subject: falcon.testing.TestCase.api_class variable
truth: The deprecated falcon.testing.TestCase.api_class class variable was removed.
quote: The deprecated ``falcon.testing.TestCase.api_class`` class variable was removed.

subject: falcon.testing.TestBase class
truth: The deprecated falcon.testing.TestBase class was removed.
quote: The deprecated ``falcon.testing.TestBase`` class was removed.

subject: falcon.testing.TestResource class
truth: The deprecated falcon.testing.TestResource class was removed.
quote: The deprecated ``falcon.testing.TestResource`` class was removed.

subject: Request.protocol property
truth: The deprecated protocol property was removed from the Request class.
quote: The deprecated ``protocol`` property was removed from the

subject: Request.get_param_as_dict() method
new: Request.get_param_as_json()
truth: The deprecated get_param_as_dict() method was removed from Request; use get_param_as_json() instead.
quote: The deprecated ``get_param_as_dict()`` method alias was removed from the

subject: custom router add_route() method_map argument
new: call falcon.routing.map_http_methods directly
truth: The add_route() method for custom routers no longer takes a method_map argument; call falcon.routing.map_http_methods directly instead.
quote: The ``add_route()`` method for custom routers no longer takes a `method_map`

subject: serialize() and deserialize() media handler signatures
new: serialize() gets content_type; deserialize(stream, content_type, content_length)
truth: serialize() now receives an extra content_type argument; deserialize() now takes stream, content_type, and content_length instead of a single raw argument.
quote: `content_type` argument, while the ``deserialize()`` method now takes

from_fact: serialize() gets content_type; deserialize(stream, content_type, content_length)

subject: falcon.routing.create_http_method_map()
truth: The deprecated falcon.routing.create_http_method_map() method was removed.
quote: The deprecated ``falcon.routing.create_http_method_map()`` method was

subject: auto_parse_qs_csv default
new: False
truth: RequestOptions.auto_parse_qs_csv now defaults to False instead of True.
quote: :attr:`~falcon.RequestOptions.auto_parse_qs_csv` now defaults to ``False``

subject: HTTPRequestEntityTooLarge class
new: HTTPPayloadTooLarge
truth: HTTPRequestEntityTooLarge was renamed to HTTPPayloadTooLarge.
quote: The ``HTTPRequestEntityTooLarge`` class was renamed to

subject: plus character in request path
old: plus character unquoted in request path
new: plus character no longer unquoted in request path, only in query string
truth: The '+' character is no longer unquoted/decoded in the request path; it is only unquoted in the query string. Existing code that expects '+' in the path to be treated as a space will silently break.
quote: The plus character (``'+'``) is no longer unquoted in the request path, but

from_fact: plus character unquoted in request path
plus character no longer unquoted in request path, only in query string

subject: disallowed character in static file paths
old: \ufffd character allowed in requested static file paths
new: \ufffd character disallowed in requested static file paths
truth: The U+FFFD (replacement character) is now disallowed in requested static file paths; requests containing it will be rejected instead of accepted.
quote: The ``\ufffd`` character is now disallowed in requested static file paths.

from_fact: \ufffd character allowed in requested static file paths
\ufffd character disallowed in requested static file paths

subject: falcon.Request.remote_addr
old: returned None when REMOTE_ADDR was missing from WSGI environ
new: assumes '127.0.0.1' when REMOTE_ADDR is missing
truth: Stop checking for None from remote_addr; when REMOTE_ADDR is absent from WSGI environ, Falcon now returns '127.0.0.1' instead of None.
quote: When the ``'REMOTE_ADDR'`` field is not present in the WSGI environ, Falcon will assume

from_fact: returned none when remote_addr was missing from wsgi environ

subject: Response header methods
new: no longer coerces header name to string
truth: Header-related methods of Response no longer call str() on the passed header name.
quote: passed header name to a string via ``str()``.

from_fact: no longer coerces header name to string

subject: Response.stream_len property
truth: The deprecated stream_len property was removed from Response; use set_stream() or content_length instead.
quote: The deprecated ``stream_len`` property was removed from the :class:`~falcon.Response` class.

subject: Response.body and HTTPStatus.body
new: text
truth: Response.body and HTTPStatus.body are renamed to text; the old name is deprecated but still available.
quote: Rename :attr:`falcon.Response.body` and :attr:`falcon.HTTPStatus.body` to ``text``.

subject: Empty body deserialization in media handler
new: raises MediaNotFoundError rendered as 400, or uses default value from get_media
truth: Deserializing an empty body now raises MediaNotFoundError (rendered as 400) instead of returning None; pass a default to get_media to suppress.
quote: a default value to ``get_media`` to be used in case of empty body.

from_fact: raises medianotfounderror rendered as 400, or uses default value from get_media

subject: falcon.testing.simulate_request params_csv
new: params_csv now defaults to False
truth: Stop relying on simulate_request to parse query string CSV params by default; set params_csv=True explicitly if you need CSV parsing.
quote: The ``params_csv`` parameter now defaults to ``False`` in

subject: falcon.HTTPError.to_json
new: to_json now returns bytes
truth: Stop treating falcon.HTTPError.to_json() output as a string; it now returns bytes.
quote: The :meth:`falcon.HTTPError.to_json` now returns ``bytes`` instead of ``str``.

subject: falcon.API class
new: falcon.App
truth: Stop using falcon.API; use falcon.App instead. The old name is deprecated and will be removed.
quote: The deprecated ``falcon.testing.TestCase.api`` property was removed.

subject: Request.get_param_as_bool accepted values
new: now also accepts 't', 'y' for True and 'f', 'n' for False
truth: get_param_as_bool now treats 't' and 'y' as True, 'f' and 'n' as False, which may cause previously-invalid values to silently become valid booleans.
quote: values for ``True``, as well as ``'f'`` and ``'n'`` for ``False``.

from_fact: now also accepts 't', 'y' for true and 'f', 'n' for false

subject: CompiledRouter route compilation timing
new: routes compiled on first request by default (compile=True to restore old behavior)
truth: CompiledRouter now defers route compilation until the first request instead of at add_route time; pass compile=True to add_route to compile immediately.
quote: passing ``compile=True`` to :meth:`falcon.routing.CompiledRouter.add_route`.

from_fact: routes compiled on first request by default (compile=true to restore old behavior)
